news.title: Galliford Try, Itaconix, Quantum Blockchain, EMV Capital, Quadrise, Ilika - Small Cap Snapshot
news.summary: Galliford Try (LSE:GFRD) has secured a place on the £1.5 billion YORbuild Major Works 2 framework, covering public sector projects across Yorkshire, the North East and the Midlands. The appointment runs for four years across two project size categories. Itaconix (AIM:ITX) has extended its collaboration with Bonals Technologies until December 2028 to push plastic-free detergent tablets into the US and Canadian markets. The company says its 8-gram dish detergent tablet is already generating strong interest from purpose-driven brands. Quantum Blockchain Technologies (AIM:QBT) has produced the first versions of its Method C AI Oracle using data from an ASIC manufacturer's Bitcoin mining rig. Live testing is now the next step once performance hits the required level. EMV Capital (AIM:EMVC) has taken part in a £14 million funding round for portfolio company Dogtooth Technologies, which makes AI-powered fruit-picking robots. The cash will accelerate commercial deployments across the UK and international markets. Quadrise (AIM:QED) has raised £1.2 million in a placing and opened a retail offer for up to the same amount again. Proceeds will fund marine trials with MSC and Cargill and projects in Morocco and the Americas. Ilika (AIM:IKA) says its retail offer was oversubscribed, raising around £500,000 as part of a wider £5 million raise. The money will support the commercial launch of its Stereax solid-state battery technology. Follow us and subscribe on YouTube, our social channels, and on proactiveinvestors.co.uk.

news.title: Apple loses challenges against EU rules to curb Big Tech
news.summary: Apple lost its challenge on Wednesday against landmark EU rules that designate its app stores and operating system iOS as gatekeepers subject to obligations ‌aimed at giving rivals more room to compete.  The EU Digital Markets Act, which sets out a ‌list of dos and don'ts for Big Tech with the threat of fines of as much as 10% of a company's global annual ​turnover, has triggered legal challenges by Apple, Meta and ByteDance since it took effect in May 2023.  The ruling by the Luxembourg-based General Court will strengthen the position of EU antitrust regulators as they attempt to make space for rivals and give Europeans more choice.

news.title: One Great Chart: Will Anyone Blink in the AI Spending War?

Wall Street analysts estimate that combined capital spending by Google, Microsoft, Amazon and Meta Platforms surged 74% year over year to hit $168 billion in the June-ending quarter, according to consensus estimates from Visible Alpha.
